body:
Resolves https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/4369

I am curious what others think. The reason why 86400.0 is valid is because it can be converted to `Int` (which happens under the hood). So if users do math on their iteration interval they will have to manually convert it to `Int`.
title: Require `interval::Int` in IterationInterval?

body: This PR refactors `Oceananigans.fields(::HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel)` so that it only contains non-nothing fields. To do this we add a function `constructor_field_names` which basically returns the same thing as `keys(fields(model))`, but before the model is constructed.
title: Refactor hydrostatic free surface names

body:
This PR avoids storing the offset in a field to be passed to a kernel and reuses the `OffsetStaticSize` type (as done for `StaticSize` in KA) so that the offsets are inferrable by the compiler and this seems to reduce the register usage.

This seems to remove the performance hit for the nonhydrostatic model that we see in #4365
For reference, here are the two different profiles:
Oceananigans main: 
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/jy2wbs7wrgqar5d62ysn5/oceananigans_96.nsys-rep?rlkey=scx32srmj6peymz1g00y9t6kf&st=2tgral26&dl=0
This PR
https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/qgf1ydrcm9i5ppr5z2tcq/oceananigans_ss-correct-kernel.nsys-rep?rlkey=3qye1njd56ec2fy17sznznek9&st=uq8gxj95&dl=0

In theory this should close #4365 because it recovers the previous performance of kernels launched without `exclude_periphery` but I would prefer to keep #4365 open until we have a test to benchmark performance
title: Remove the performance hit from using `KernelOffsets`

body: This fixes a bug in the `summary` method for `ConjugateGradientPoissonSolver`. It also adds a docstring. @xkykai I believe you coded this, right? Can you take a look at the docstring to make sure it's okay?
title: Fix `summary()` for `ConjugateGradientPoissonSolver`
